caption:
This week, in Elon Musk's lawsuit against Sam Altman and OpenAI, the jury saw video of a sworn deposition from Mira Murati, OpenAI's former CTO. Elaborating on her comments in our latest @newyorkermag story, she alleged that Altman was not always truthful, gave different executives conflicting information, and pitted them against each other. 

Over the course of an 18-month investigation, we traced the yearslong history of these claims from Altman's colleagues, and looked at what this tells us about integrity in AI leadership, and how it affects all of us.

Link in bio to read the full investigation.

#ai #samaltman #openai #elonmusk #news
audio_track_actual: Ronan Farrow, on camera, narrating excerpts from his and Andrew Marantz's April 2026 New Yorker investigation of Sam Altman and OpenAI.
video_overlay_text_observed: Talking-head footage of Farrow speaking to camera, intercut with on-screen quotes from his article and supporting graphics (named executives, dates, the New Yorker cover).
audio_content_summary: Farrow narrates several documented threads from the published investigation: Altman's 2023 accusation that Anthropic co-founders Dario and Daniela Amodei were plotting a coup at OpenAI (an accusation Altman later denied making, after a senior executive contradicted his account); senior Microsoft executives describing Altman as having 'misrepresented, distorted, renegotiated, reneged on agreements,' citing an OpenAI–Amazon deal alleged to conflict with Microsoft's exclusivity terms; a U.S.-government meeting in which Altman pitched intelligence officials on a Chinese 'AGI Manhattan Project' and, when pressed for evidence, said only that he had 'heard things' before never following up; reported quotes from Y Combinator founder Paul Graham and from Aaron Swartz (in 2013, prior to his suicide) characterizing Altman's relationship to the truth; and the November 2023 OpenAI board firing, during which Altman reportedly told the board, 'I can't change my personality,' when pressed to acknowledge a pattern of deception and people-pleasing.
implied_frame: This video marks a pivot in the project's framing: the systems built across the prior eras are largely anonymous, but Era V's reckoning is about specific people in specific rooms, with specific words said on the record and recorded. Farrow's piece is the first such record on Altman.
capture_method: Headless browser recording of the in-page <video> element via MediaRecorder over captureStream(), viewport 1280x800.
